steps.title: Identify the logical gap the blank must fill
steps.content: The passage establishes two facts: (1) handshakes create trust between people, and (2) the team wants to improve human-robot interactions. The blank must complete the sentence "The researchers want the robot's handshake to feel realistic because ______" — the answer must connect a realistic handshake to improved human-robot interactions via that trust-building function.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — lifelike handshakes may make people more comfortable interacting with robots.: Directly connects the realism of the handshake to improved human-robot interaction through the trust/comfort mechanism stated in the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — it's easier to program a robot to perform handshakes than it is to program a robot to perform some other types of greetings.: The passage never compares handshakes to other greetings or discusses programming difficulty. ✗
  • Choice C — people are less likely to interact with robots that don't look like humans.: The passage is about handshake feel, not robot appearance; visual likeness is never discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — the robot in the researchers' study may have uses other than interacting with humans.: The passage describes no other use for the robot; this introduces an unsupported claim. ✗
That matches Choice A — lifelike handshakes may make people more comfortable interacting with robots..
